ety: S. वर्जनीयं, rt. वृज् or वृच्
def: v.n. To be saved, to save oneself, escape (from, -se), effect (one's) escape; to be saved, spared, set aside, laid by; to remain over, be left; to be spared, to recover, survive, live; to turn away (from, -se), shrink (from); to avoid, shirk, fly (from)
more: v. n. विज् to separate. 1. To remain; to be over; to be left. Apne se bache, to aur ko deṅ . From needs of self whate'er is o'er may go to form another's store. 2. To be saved, spared, laid by. Kauṛī nahīṅ bachī, sārī tankhuāh ghāo ghap ho gaī . 3. To escape; make or effect one's escape; come out safe; to be saved. 1. Jachchā, bachchā donoṅ bacheṅ . Benediction. May the lying-in-mother and her child both live! 2. Jān bachī, lākhoṅ pāë . Prov. Escaped with life, millions found. 3. Ab-ke bache to ghar ghar rache . Prov. Spared this once, in every house the light of joy shall shine. 4. Yā Khudā khair! bachā hāth pair! Prov. Heaven preserve us, life and limb. 4. To recover; survive; live; to be alive. Bhādoṅ se bache to phir mileṅge . Prov. Escaped this August we shall meet again. 5. To escape from; shirk; elude; evade. 6. To hold back; keep from or aloof; abstain; eschew; avoid; shrink; run…
v. n. To be saved, be spared, to escape, avoid, remain unexpended, recover.
subs: v.n. To be or remain over; — to be on (one's) guard (against, -se), take care (against), avoid
v.n. To get clean away, escape in safety.

more: n. m. S. वद् to speak. 1. Debate; discussion; dispute. 2. Contention; strife; rencounter; fight. 3. A law-suit; action; case; trial. 4. Alloy; mixture of a baser with a superior metal. To contest a matter; contend for; bring an action ( muqadma laṛānā ). 3. Hin. Wom. To retort; give impertinent or crooked answers. Maiṅ kyā tere barābar kī hūṅ, jo mujh se bādā kartā hai . Wom. 4. To contend, engage, cope or fight with. Sapehrā bhasam lagāë āṅg ko, bidyā saṅg lāyā, Gauṛ Baṅgāle bīch bād karne kyoṅ āyā? Play. bād-ě-murād. s.f. Fair wind, favourable wind
adj. Wandering, vagrant; senseless, unmeaning; useless; — s.f. Senselessness; uselessness, fruitlessness; waste.

def: s. plur. (of بديعه) Rare things.
s. See بط bat̤.
s.m. Corr. of بطك bat̤ak, q.v.
more: s. (dakh.) A duck: see بطخ bat̤aḵẖ.

def: prop. n. Name of a country, near the source of the Oxus, famous for its rubies.

def: s.m. Cloud; sponge
more: n. m. badrī, badlī , n. f. S. वारिद from वारि water, दा to give. A cloud. bādal phaṭnā , v. n. To be dispersed (the clouds); to become clear (the sky) ( khulnā ). bādal chhānā , v. n. To lower. See abar chhānā .
subs: Clouds to disperse, to become clear (the sky)
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بادل (badl /⁠bādal⁠/) (c. 1503) / بدل (bdl /⁠badal⁠/) (c. 1434), from Prakrit 𑀯𑀤𑁆𑀤𑀮 (vaddala), from Sanskrit वार्दल (vārdala).

def: s.m. Change, alteration; exchange, substitution; return, requital, retaliation; — postp. In exchange (for, -ke, or -se); in return (for, -ke); on account (of); for
more: s. m. Exchange, substitution, retaliation; (postpos.) In exchange, for. بدل لينا badal lenā, To exchange, to take in exchange. بدل مال badal-i-māl, s. m. Price, barter.
subs: (-ke sāth, or -se), To make or effect an exchange (with, or for); to exchange or substitute (for)
s.m. Stipend given in money or kind to public or private servants.

def: s.f. = badlī, bādal, q.q.v. s.f. Silk cloth embroidered with silver, brocade ( = bādlā, q.v.). s.m. Gold or silver cloth, brocade of silken stuff variegated ( = lappā); gold or silver thread.

v.n. To be changed or altered; to change, alter, vary; to assume another form, grow or become; to shift, veer, turn round; to be removed, transferred, transplanted; to change for the worse, look older; to lose colour, fade; to be disfigured; — v.t. To change, alter; to exchange, barter; to substitute one thing for another; to disguise; to place a false construction on, to misrepresent, pervert, torture, twist; to transfer, remove, transplant; to transform, transmute; to shift (one's ground, &c.), shuffle (generally with the word bāt, e.g. bāt badaltā hai)
more: v. n. 1. To change; alter; vary; shift; chop and change; veer; turn round. Hawā hī badal gaī . 2. To be changed; to assume another form; grow or become; turn into. 3. To be removed, transplanted, transferred. 4. To look worse, older, etc; lose color; to be disfigured. Tumhārī to bilkul sūrat hī badal gaī .

v. a. 1. To change; alter; make different; convert. 2. To barter; exchange. Lohe ke chane badal do . 3. To put one thing for another; substitute; commute. 4. To disguise; put a false coloring or construction upon. Bhes badalnā . 5. To transfer; transplant; transpose. Ek jagah se dūsrī jagah badalnā . 6. To transform; transmute. Sūrat badalnā . 7. To shuffle; shift; prevaricate; equivocate. Bāt badaltā hai? dānā badlī kar'nā , v. n. lit . To take the grain, as pigeons, out of each other's bills. Hence, to bill and coo.
v. a. To change, exchange, alter.
subs: v.n. = badalnā (and is the more common form)
v.t. To take in exchange, to exchange = badalnā.
wety: From بَدَل (badal) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: oblique plural of بادَل (bādal, “cloud”)
vocative plural of بادَل (bādal)
s.f. = badlāʼī, q.v. s.f. Barter; exchange; price of exchange; something given in exchange ( = badalwāʼī).
v.t. To cause to be exchanged; to have, or get, changed or altered; to change, &c. (see badalnā).
more: v. a. Caus. of [بدلنا q. v.
ex: اُس نے مُجْھ سے یہ سات دَفْعَہ بَدَلْوائی۔
us ne mujh se ye sāt daf'a badalvāī.
She got it changed by me seven times.
wety: Causative form of بَدَلْنا (badalnā) with stem augmented by وا (vā)

def: s.m. = bādām, q.v. s.m. Almond, Amygdalus communis; an Indian fruit resembling the almond, Terminalia catappa (the catapang of the Malay countries, called, in India, the country almond).
s.m. = bādām, q.v. s.m. Almond, Amygdalus communis; an Indian fruit resembling the almond, Terminalia catappa (the catapang of the Malay countries, called, in India, the country almond).
s.m. Almond, Amygdalus communis; an Indian fruit resembling the almond, Terminalia catappa (the catapang of the Malay countries, called, in India, the country almond).
more: n. m. S. वाताद. An almond; an Indian fruit resembling the almond; Terminalia catappa . Wat.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَادَام (bādām).

def: s.m. A silkworm in the aurelia or chrysalis state; — a kind of silk; a faqir's dress made of rags sewn together.
more: s. m. 1. A silkworm in the aurelia or chrysalis state. 2. A sort of silk. 3. A dervisʼs garment formed of patches.

def: n. m. 1. The aurelia or silkworm in its chrysalis state. 2. A kind of silk. 3. A faqir's dress made of rags sewn together.

adj. & s.m. Almond-coloured; almond-shaped; composed of or containing almonds; — a dish of which almonds form the chief ingredient; a eunuch (particularly one born with a rudimentary penis); a kind of rice
more: adj. Almond-colored; almond shaped. bādāmī āṅkh , n. f. Small oval eyes.
n. m. 1. A dish of which almonds form a main ingredient. 2. A eunuch born with a rudimentary penis . 3. A species of rice.
adj. 1. Almond-coloured, almond-shaped. 2. A kind of eunuch.
subs: s.f. Almond-shaped eye; placid, sleepy eye.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بادامی (bādāmī). By surface analysis, بادام (bādām) + ـی (-ī).

def: adj. Almond-coloured; having the flavour of almonds; — s.f. A species of rice.
s.m. Mouth; face, countenance.
more: n. m. 1. The body; trunk; frame; flesh and bones. 2. The privities; pudendum feminœ. badan phal jānā , v. n. To be covered with any eruption, as pustules, boils, itch, etc. badan ṭūṭnā , v. n. 1. To feel one's bones ache. 2. To stretch or bend one's body. badan ṭornā , v. a. To take athletic exercises. insān kā badan . The human body. G. G.
s. m. The mouth, face, countenance.
s. m. The body. بدن پهل جانا badan phal jānā, v. n. To be covered with an eruption of pustules.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَدَن (badan), borrowed from Arabic بَدَن (badan).

def: s.m. Body; privities
subs: v.n. To be covered with an eruption of pustules, &c.
v.n. To suffer racking pains in the body, or in the bones; to have the body stretched or bent; to have the body trained by gymnastic exercises
To train the body by gymnastic or athletic exercises.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَدَن (badan), borrowed from Arabic بَدَن (badan).

def: (for ba-āṅ), adv. lit. 'By that'; thereby, therefrom, thence.
adj. Of or relating to the body (badan); corporeal.
s.m. The egg-plant, Solanum melongenā (syn. baigan).
more: s. The egg plant (Solanum melongena): see بيگن baigan.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بادنجان from Sanskrit वातिङ्गण (vātiṅgaṇa, “the plant that cures the wind”) from Dravidian, ultimately from Proto-Dravidian *waẓVtV. Doublet of بینگن (baiṅgan).

def: oblique plural of بَدَن (badan)
s. m. Altercation.
adj. = vādānya, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Munificent, liberal, generous.

def: adj. & s.m. Made of cotton; — the cotton plant.

more: adv. Out; outside. badar kar'nā , v. a. To put out of doors; drive away; banish ( bāhar k . 1, 2.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بدر (ba-dar), from به (ba) + در (dar).

def: adj. Out of doors, without, outside, out
subs: s.f. lit. 'going out'; outlet or channel for water; drain, sewer; — badar-rau-ě-ʻām, s.f. Public sewer: badar-rau nikālnā, v.n. To make an outlet or channel for water; to make a drain
v.t. To turn out, expel, eject, banish
v.n. To show a balance due; to debit (to)
s.f. Striking out objectionable items or excessive charges from an account; audit of an account; taxing an overcharged bill; — a record of fines and impositions on the clerks or writers of an office.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بدر (ba-dar), from به (ba) + در (dar).

def: s.m. Cotton; the plant Mimosa octandra; a certain medicinal drug.
s.f. The jujube tree (see badar); the cotton shrub; — name of one of the sources of the Ganges in the Himalaya range, a celebrated place of pilgrimage
subs: s.m. A kind of perfume, a leaf
s.f. Name of a plant, a variety of the Śephalica or Nyctanthes with blue flowers
s.f. A species of jujube tree; — a kind of perfume (apparently a dried shell-fish)

s.m. lit. 'Rock of Badarī'; name of a rocky eminence at the source of the Ganges ( = Badrī), the Badrī-nāth of modern travellers; or a town and temple on the west bank of the Alaka-nanda river in the province of Śrīnagar
s.m. Name of a temple at Badarī; also = badrī-śail, q.v.

subs: s.m. Name of a hermitage, &c. ( = badrī-śail).

def: adv. For insertion or entry; by or according to the entry; per invoice.
adv. In or to a degree; by many degrees; ever-so-much, greatly, vastly
n. f. Writing off items of an account which are objectionable or excessive; audit of an account; taxing an overcharged bill.
s.f. See s.v. ba-dar. s.m. A large house; a granary raised on piles.
more: n. f. A drain; sewer. badar-rau-i-am , A public drain. badar-rau nikāl'nā, morī banānā , v. a. To make a drain or channel; drain; let out water, etc. badar nikālnā , v. a. To show a balance due; to debit.
s. f. A drain, a sewer.

def: adv. In hand; to hand.
adv. According to custom; according to rule or practice; in the usual manner, as usual, as before.
more: adv. According to rule or practice; as usual; as before.
As usual, according to custom, in the manner, customarily.

def: adv. By the good fortune (of, -kī), by the favour or bounty (of); by means (of), through, owing (to).
more: adv. By the grace or bounty of; under the auspices or good graces of; by means of; through; by dint of; owing to. Tumhārī ba-daulat roṭī khāte haiṅ .
adv. By favor of; by means of; under the auspices of. Āp kī ba-daulat roṭī kamā khāte haiṅ .
By the fortune, by the bounty, by the means: in or with empire or fortune.
wety: Compound of ب (ba) + دولت (daulat), both borrowed from Classical Persian.

def: s.m. A Brāhman; — submarine fire
adj. & s.m. Of the desert; — an Arab of the desert; a countryman, country-bumpkin.
more: adj. Of the country or desert; an Arab of the desert, a countryman.

def: s.f. Beginning, commencement.
— See s.v. بد for all comp. beginning with bad.
adj. 1. Ill-starred; unlucky; unfortunate. 2. Wretched; miserable; doomed; accursed.
Mah. Wom. n. m. A firebrand; mischief-maker; wretch. Paṛī rahtī hai thutkārī sī merī jān ke pīchhe, Mujhe jīne nahīṅ degī, bahan hai bad-balā terī . Wom. Rāhat.

n. m. A sail. bād-i-samūm , Ped.; H. lū , n. f. A hot wind like the blast from a furnace; the simoom. bād-i-mukhālif , n. f. Ped. for H. ulṭī hawā . Contrary winds. bād-i-muāfiq , Ped.; H. sīdhī hawā , n. f. Favourable winds.
more: s. m. 1. A sail. 2. A vessel used to hold a lamp for the purpose of protecting it from the wind.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بادبان (bādbān).

def: adj. Internally evil; false-hearted.
stink, stench; bad-smell, bad-odor
ex: تمہاری بغلوں سے بدبو آ رہی ہے
tumhārī baġalõ se bad-bū ā rhī he!
there is a stench coming off your armpits!
اپنے بستر کی چادروں سے بدبو صاف کریں۔
apnī bistar kī cādarõ se bad-bū sāf karẽ
clean the stench off your bedsheets!
wety: From Classical Persian بَد بو (bad-bū, “stink, stench”).

def: n. f. Bad or offensive smell; fetor; stink; stench ( dur-gandh ).
stinky, smelly; reeking
ex: اپنی بدبودار کمرہ کو صاف کرو
apnī badbūdār kamra ko sāf karo
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَد بُودَار (bad būdār, “stinky, smelly”), analyzable as بد (bad, “bad, disagreeable”) + بو (bū, “smell, odor”) + دار (dār, “having”).

s.m. Cloud = bādal, q.v. s.m. Cloud; sponge
more: (for بادل q. v.) s. m. A cloud.
subs: Clouds to disperse, to become clear (the sky)

def: n. m. S. वध् to kill. Slaughter; killing; murder; destruction. Āj kal Hindustān meṅ baṛā gaū-baddh hotā hai . Hin. Now-a-days there is great slaughter of cows.
more: n. f. S. विधि from विधा to arrange. 1. God ( bidhnā ). 2. The moon ( chāṅd ). Bidhi dāhine, dukkh binasāve, Vohī bāṅë hoë, dukh baḍhāve . Chaupāī. Bihārī Lāl. The moon upon your right, your pain is on the wane, But turned towards your left the moon increaseth pain. 3. A sacred precept; a law; rule; order; decree. 4. Custom; usage; manner; way; mode. 5. State; condition; case; situation. Jā bidhi rākhe Rām, tā hī bidh rahīye . Prov. Be content in the state in which Ram has placed you. 6. Kind; sort; make; fashion. 7. A conjunction of auspicious stars for the bride and bridegroom. 8. Balance of an account; adjustment of an account. bidh khā'nā yā mil'nā, mī'jān paṭnā v. n. 1. See ittifāq h . 2. To be struck (a bargain). bidh milānā , v. a. 1. To consult the stars, especially the horoscope of the bride and bridegroom before tying them in wedlock. Chalo hamāre sāth kahīṅ jā-ke tum bidh milwā…

n. m. S. बुद्धि from बुध् to know, Pālī. buddho . 1. Knowledge ( gyān ); intelligence; understanding ( samajh ); sense; intellect; perception; judgment; wisdom ( dānāī ). 2. Quickness of apprehension; discrimination; discernment; capacity ( buddhī ); cleverness; skill. Honhār hirde base, bisar jāt sub sudh, Jaisī hot hotabbitā, taisī upje budh . Dohā. The evil in our fate its shadow casts before, Its image occupies the mind, all sense forlore. 3. (S. बुद्ध ) A wise man; the ninth incarnation of Vishnu, and the apparent founder of Buddhism. 4. ( Ast .) Mercury (A. utārid ). 5. ( budh-wār ) Wednesday (P. chahārshambah ).
1. s. f. A sacred precept, rule, order, law, statute, direction, decree, injunction. 2. Manner, mode, kind, sort. 3. s. m. Name of Brahmā, providence.
s. f. Sense, knowledge, wisdom, understanding, intellect, discretion.
s. m. 1. A wise or learned man, a sage. 2. The planet Mercury. 3. Wednesday. ¶ बुद्ध buddha, The ninth avatār or Hindū incarnation.
wety: Shortened from بدھوار (budhvār), from Sanskrit बुधवार (budhavāra).

s.m. One who governs or suppresses his wrath

more: adj. S. वृध्द, Pālī buḍḍho . Old; advanced in years. buḍḍhā , n. m. An old man ( būṛhā ). Ham buḍḍhe huë to kyā, jī to nahīṅ buḍḍhā huā . Prov. Saying. What though I'm old in years, my heart's still young.

def: s.m. One who is bound; captive, prisoner.
n. m. Executioner; butcher; huntsman; fowler ( chiṛī-mār ). Jūṅ baddhak se gāë ḍare saë, so gat bhaī hamārī . Play. Chaubolā . I fear you as the ox fears the butcher.
s.f. See s.v. baddhā. adj. Bound, tied; suppressed, checked, restrained, withheld, obstructed; fixed, firmly rooted
subs: (baddha+aṅjulī), s.f. Respectful salutation, putting the joined hands to the forehead by way of salutation
adj. & s.m. Young; — pupil; — child whose hair has not been shaven
s.f. Name of a pungent root, a kind of garlic
s.m. One who governs or suppresses his wrath

def: s.f. An ornament worn round the neck, a long chain or garland of flowers worn (as by a bride and bridegroom) round the neck, hanging down to the waist, and crossing behind and before; a similar ornament made of leather; belt, sash; pair of braces; brace (of a carriage, &c.); streak, weal, mark of a stroke with a cane, &c.
more: s. f. An ornament worn round the neck, hanging down to the waist and crossing behind and before; a belt, a sash, brace (of a carriage, &c.).

n. f. Unfair dealing; fraud; dishonesty; corrupt practices. bad-diyānatī se , adv. Mala fide ; with fraudulent intent; dishonestly.
n. f. Curse; imprecation; malediction. bad-duā denā; H. sarāpnā , v. a. To curse; call down curses; heap curses on ( kosnā ).
wety: From بد (“mal-, evil, inauspicious”) + دعا (“prayer, wish”).

def: adv. Late; after a long time, after much delay.
Evil doer.

n. f. Speaking ill of one; slander; detraction; backbiting. Jo koī kare yāroṅ kī bad-goī, Uske bans meṅ rahe na koī! Rahe to koī andhā, laṅgṛā, lūlā, koṛhī! The prologue to a drinking bout of Bhaṅgaṛs . If any dare speak ill of us, May all his race be cut off thus, Or stricken blind, lame, maimed, leprous!
adj. Suspicious; mistrustful; disaffected. bad-gumānī, bad-zanī , n. f. Suspicion; mistrust; distrust.
s.m. Prohibition, hindrance, opposition, obstruction; annoyance; damage: danger; cord or tape of a bedstead ( = nawāṛ, &c.); smaller division of an estate; plain, desert.
s.f. Stubble.
s. f. Stubble.
s.m. Increase, augmentation, advance, progress, premium.

s. f. Pain, distress: obstruction, hinderance, prevention.
wety: From Sanskrit बाधा (bādhā).

subs: s.m. Punishment of death, capital punishment
badh-sthal, s.m. badh-sthalī, s.f. Place of execution, or of slaughter
s.f. Desire, or intent, to kill or hurt
adj. Wishing for death, desirous of dying
(-kā, or -ko), To kill, murder, slaughter ( = badhnā)
(badha+upāya), s.m. Instrument or means of putting to death
vulg. badhuʼans, s.m. Slaughter of a whole race or family; wholesale slaughter, butchery, utter destruction; — badh-waṅś karnā, v.t. To slaughter wholesale, to butcher, destroy, exterminate
adj. Deserving of death; fit to be killed or slaughtered.

def: s.f. = badhāwā, q.v. s.m. Congratulations on the birth of a child; presents distributed at the ceremony of cutting the umbilical cord, or at the birth of a child; presents carried to the house of a woman on the sixth or the fortieth day after childbirth; song of congratulation, congratulations; merry-making, revelry; — badhāwā denā, To congratulate, &c.
more: n. f. badhāvā ; Garh. baḍau , n. m. H. baṛhnā to increase. 1. Increase; growth; development (baṛhotrī) . 2. Propagation of the species (byāṅt) . 3. Child; offspring; hope of issue. Wom. 4. Congratulation on the birth of a child. 5. Revelry; enjoyment; pleasure ( maṅgal ). 6. Congratulations ( mubārkī ); a song of congratulation ( ānand-badhāvā ). Hin. Wom. Nand grah utre Kanhāī, Vā din ghar ghar bajī badhāī . Behāri Lāl. 7. Congratulatory gifts; presents to the servants at births and marriages. Ek ko sāī, dūsre ko badhāī . Prov. Earnest money to one, the job (or gift) to another. 8. Presents, etc. carried to the house of a woman on the sixth or fortieth day after childbirth ( badhāvā ). E. badhāī de'nā . v. a. To congratulate; to offer congratulations. badhāī gānā , v. a. To sing songs of congratulation.
wety: Borrowed from Hindi बधाई (badhāī).

def: s.f. Increase, growth, development, &c. ( = baṛhāʼī, q.v.); propagation; offspring, issue, child; hope of issue (in the language of women).

Indemnification; indemnity; reparation; restitution. 3. Reprisal; retaliation; revenge; retribution; atonement; redress; satisfaction; evil consequences. Gujar karī maiṅ missī khā-ke, rahī tarastī ghī se, Kisī janam kā badlā linā, nās gaī! mujh dhī se . Play. Chaubolā . On dry bread I am forced to live, for butter cravings vain, Perish my mother who for past birth's ills her child hath slain! ☞ She curses her mother who has married her to a poor man, in revenge possibly for ills she may have suffered at the hands of her daughter in a former birth. badlā denā , v. a. 1. To give an equivalent; requite; compensate; recompense; reward; pay for; make restitution. 2. To indemnify; make amends; make good. Gāt bajāt pī chale saj-…

v.t. To speak: to settle, appoint, ordain; to take or invoke (as a witness); to predestinate, predestine; to stipulate, agree; to vow, pledge (one's) word; to wager, bet, stake; to acknowledge, own, admit; to observe or keep (a vow, &c.); to heed, mind, regard, obey; to empannel (a jury).
more: v. a. S. वदन from वद् to speak. 1. To predestinate; predestine; preordain; doom. Hogā wohī jo qismat meṅ badā hai . Prov. Saying. Whate'er your destiny assuredly will be. 2. To appoint; nominate; enrol. Gawāh badnā . 3. To wager; lay a bet; stake. Shart badnā . 4. To vow; swear; pledge one's word; promise. Badan badī thī raṅg-mahal kī, ṭūṭī maṅḍhaīyā meṅ lāë utāro, Badan badī thī lāl palaṅg kī, ṭūṭī kaṭhulyā pe lāë biṭhāyo . Wom. Song. You swore a splendid house I'd have, a hovel you have brought me to, You swore a rich bed should be mine, and lo! this rickety bed, fico! 5. To own; acknowledge; admit. Bāṅh chhuṭāë jāt ho, nibal jān-ke moë, Hirde meṅ se jāoge, to mard badūṅgī toë . Dohā. You shake me oft and go away, for me you weak esteem: Expel your image from my mind, then man you may beseem. 6. To heed; care for; regard; count ( ginnā ). Woh mujhe kuchh nahīṅ badtā . Wom.
v. a. 1. To wager, to bet. 2. To settle, to predestinate, to take (as witness), to obey, to acknowledge, to agree, not to refuse.

s.f. The pox; St. Anthony's fire
s.m. Flatterer; boaster; (in India, generally,) a Bhāṭ, a musician or minstrel
s.m. Fan, punkah, ventilator, thermantidote; bellows
bād-ě-murād. s.f. Fair wind, favourable wind
adj. Wandering, vagrant; senseless, unmeaning; useless; — s.f. Senselessness; uselessness, fruitlessness; waste.

def: adj. Proud, vain, of vain ideas (a person).
n. f. An unlucky moment.
s.m. Poet. for bādshāh, q.v.
s.m. King, monarch, sovereign; lord, master; a proficient (in, -kā)
more: n. m. Cor. of P. pādshāh from pād a throne, shāh an owner. 1. A sovereign; king; monarch. 2. Owner; governor; proprietor; master. Apne ghar ke sab bādshāh haiṅ . Prov. Every man's house is his castle. 3. One skilled in any art (ustād, 2) . Apne kām kā bādshāh hai. bādshāh-zādā, shah-zāda , n. m. A king's son; prince. bādshāh-zādī , n. f. A princess; queen. bādshāh-kush , n. m. A regicide (the person). bādshāh-kushī; H. rāj-hattyā . n. f. Regicide (the crime).
s. m. A king, a sovereign.
subs: s.m. King's son, prince; — bādshāh-zādī, s.f. Princess
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بادشاه (bādšāh), variant of Classical Persian پادشاه (pādišāh). Compare Maithili बादसाह (bādᵊsāh) / 𑒥𑒰𑒠𑒮𑒰𑒯 (bādasāha), Punjabi ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹ (bādśāh) / بادْشاہ (bādśāh), Sindhi بادشاهُ (bādśāhu) / बादशाहु, Gujarati બાદશાહ (bādśāh), Marathi बादशहा (bādaśhā), Bengali বাদশাহ (badośah), Telugu పాదుషా (pāduṣā).

def: adj. & adv. Royal, imperial, princely; — kingly, royally, princely, in a royal or princely manner or fashion.
more: adj. Imperial, royal. adv. Royally, princely, kingly.

def: s.f. Kingdom, realm, empire, dominion, state, rule, government, sway; royalty, sovereignty
more: s. f. Royalty, empire, kingdom, government.
subs: (-kī), To rule (over), to reign, govern (syn. rāj karnā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بادشاهت (“royalty”). By surface analysis, بَادْشَاہ (bādśāh, “king, monarch”) + َت (at).

def: n. f. 1. Kingdom; sovereignty; monarchy. 2. State; government; rule. 3. Realm; empire; dominion; sceptre. bādshāhat karnā, rāj karnā , v. a. To hold the sceptre; reign; govern; rule.
adj. & s.f. Kingly, royal, regal, sovereign, imperial; — royalty, sovereignty, sway, rule ( = bādshāhat)
more: adj. Royal, imperial, regal. s. f. Royalty, empire, reign, sovereignty, office or duty of a sovereign, kingdom.
subs: s.m. Royal or princely expenditure; expenditure from the privy purse
s.f. Royal grant or charter; a written document conveying lands or titles from the ruling power; royal tenure, grant of rent-free land
s.f. Royal court, His (or Her) Majesty's court
(-meṅ), To rule (in or over), to reign ( = bādshāhat karnā)
s.m. Royal family, dynasty; royal household
s.m. Royal or state property, estates belonging to the crown.

def: adj. Kingly; royal; regal; sovereign; imperial bādshāhī haq , n. m. Royal prerogative. bādshāhī hukm , n. m. Royal mandate. bādshāhī sanad , n. f. A royal grant, or charter; a written document conveying lands or titles from the ruling power; a royal tenure, or grant of land rent-free. bādshāhī ٴadālat , n. f. His or Her majesty's court; royal court. bādshāhī gharānā, shāhī khāndān , n. m. Royal family, dynasty ( rāj-bans ). bādshāhī māl , n. m. State property; estates belonging to the crown. bādshāhī imārāt , n. f. Royal buildings. bādshāhī kharch , n. m. 1. Expenditure from the privy purse. 2. Royal or princely expenditure.
n. m. A bad omen. bad-shugūn sūrat , n. f. Ped. An ill-omened countenance; an unlucky face.
n. f. Ill treatment; discourtesy.
adj. Clumsy.

adj. 1. Ill-looking; ill-shaped or formed; illmade. 2. Inelegant; ugly; unsightly; misshapen. Phr. A forbidding countenance; a wry face. bad-sūrat karnā , v. a. To deface; disfigure; deform; spoil. bad-sūratī ; Pop. badsūrtī , n. f. Ugliness; disfigurement; defacement; distortion.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بدصورت (badsūrat), or from Urdu بد (“bad”) + Urdu صورت (“appearance”).

def: stupid, idiotic; rude, impolite, ill-mannered
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَدتَمِیز (bad-tamīz). By surface analysis, بَد (bad, “bad; un-”) + تَمِیز (tamīz, “discernment”).

def: adj. Worse; inferior; very bad
more: adj. Worse; inferior. badtar honā , v. n. To become or get worse; deteriorate.
subs: v.n. To become or get worse; to deteriorate.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بدتر (badtar).

def: adj. Evil-minded; malevolent; ill-disposed.
adj. & s.m. Infamous; — a bad character, infamous man.
more: n. m. A bad character; one who has a bad name.
s. f. 1. Beginning, appearance. 2. The desert. 3. (for بدوي) A tribe of Arabs inhabiting the desert.

def: s.f. The first (of anything); beginning, commencement; — a desert; — (for badawī), a tribe of Arabs inhabiting the desert.
corruption, malpractice
wety: From Urdu بد (“evil”) + Urdu عنوان ('nvān, “topic”).

def: s.m. Flying fox; bat.
more: W; n. f. S. वातुली. A flying fox; bat.
s. m. A flying fox, a bat.

def: adj. Low-born.

Mah. n. m. 1. A large cup of copper; a bowl. 2. Forest; wood. Ghar meṅ rahe aur jaṅgal kahlāë, Isko sāhib-zarf * hī batlāë . Riddle. Ans. bādyā . Dwells in the house, and yet a forest is its name The owner of rich pots and pans could tell the same.
s.m. Sweet fennel; anise-seed ( = sauṅf).
adj. Foul mouthed. bad-zabān , n. m. One who uses foul language. Sab chale tere āstāṅ ko chhoṛ, Bad-zabāṅ, ab to is zubāṅ ko chhoṛ . Jurat. bad-zabānī , n. f. Obscene, indecent, or foul language.
adj. 1. Low-born; low; mean; base ( kamīnah ). 2. Vicious; corrupt ( kharāb ); unprincipled.
adj. Ungraceful; inelegant; not matching; unsightly.
adj. Faithless; treacherous; false. bad-ٴahdī , n. f. Breach of promise or trust.
n. f. Misdeeds; immorality; dissoluteness.
n. f. Maladministration; misgovernment; anarchy.

s. f. Wind, air; flatulency, rheumatism. باي پچني bāʼī pachnī, To be mortified, to be depressed, to die away. باي سرنا bāʼī sarnā, see باوسرنا bāʼo sarnā. باي مين بهڙکنا bāʼī meṅ bhaṛaknā, To prate foolishly.
n. f. ( Physics ) Wind.
s. m. Name of a medicinal seed.
s. m. See باوگولا bāʼo golā.
s.m. See s.v. bāʼī or bāʼe. s.f. Lady, mistress, madam; woman, wife (local; cf. bāʼīkā); — dancing-girl, harlot; mother bawd, la bonne
subs: s.f. Mother bawd, the old abbess (syn. nāyikā).

def: Māṛ. n. m. Wom. 1. Share; portion; allotted portion. E. 2. W. Presents made to elder women on the husband's side. 3. E. Sweetmeats, etc. distributed to friends in marriage feasts, or in celebrating a success.
s.m. Presents of sweetmeats, &c., forming part of an offering to a deity, or prepared on festive occasions, such as marriages, &c., and sent to friends and acquaintances; — share, allotted portion.

r: bāëṅ-bāëṅ karnā, bāṅ-bāṅ, yā bhauṅ bhauṅ k
def: v. a. 1. W. To prate; talk nonsense. Apnī hī bāëṅ kīye jātā hai, aur kī bhī suntā hai? 2. W. To make a hell of a noise; yell; howl. Bāeṅ-bāeṅ kar-ke jān khā gayā . 3. E. To betray a secret. Chhipī bāt ko kyoṅ bān-bāṅ karte ho? E.
= bāṅ-bāṅ, q.v. s.f. Prating, jabber, senseless talk
subs: To prate, talk foolishly, &c.; to blurt out (a secret, &c.); to make a disagreeable noise, bellow, howl.

def: adv. In virtue of; in consideration of; by reason of; according to; agreeably. ba-etebār ohdah , In virtue of one's office; ex-officio .
adv. In respect (of, -ke), by reason (of), in consideration (of), in virtue (of); according (to), agreeably (to).

part. adj. Weaving; weaver; woven; (used as last member of compounds, e.g. kinārī-bāf, s.m. A weaver of edging or lace; — zarī-bāf, s.m. Gold-lace weaver); — s.f. Cloth interwoven with gold, gold tissue.
more: (from بافتن) part. act. and pass. used in compos. Weaver, woven. کناري باف kinārī-bāf, A weaver of lace. زري باف zarī-bāf, 1. A gold lace weaver. 2. Gold tissue, cloth interwoven with gold.

def: s.m. Scurf, dandriff (syn. rūsī).
more: s. f. A kind of scales or dandruff formed in the head.

def: s.m. Weaver.
hypothetically speaking; entertain an unlikely situation
ex: اور اگر، بفرض محال، کوئی شخص یہ کہتا ہے کہ جتنی عمارتیں دنیا میں موجود ہیں انہیں کسی نے نہیں بنایا تو روئے زمین پر، مشرق سے مغرب تک کوئی بھی انسان اس بات کو تسلیم نہیں کرے گا۔

And if, hypothetically speaking, a person says [claiming] that all the buildings that exist were not made by anyone, then on the face of the earth, from the East to the West, no human would be willing to accept this statement.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَه فَرْضِ مُحَال (ba farz-i muhāl). By surface analysis, بَ (ba, “in”) + فَرْضِ (farz-i, “example; instance of”) + مُحال (muhāl, “inconceivable; impossible”), literally “in the case of the inconceivable”.

def: adv. At a distance (of); at intervals; far-removed.
adv. Instantly ( = fiʼl-faur and fauraṉ, q.q.v.).
adj. (dakh.) Fat (exceeding), gross ( Harris ).
part. = bāfta, q.v.; — s.f. Weaving; web, tissue, texture. s.m. A kind of silk cloth; — buttons woven like mohair; — a colour in pigeons.

ety: of bāftan; rt. Zend vap; S. vap
def: s.m. A kind of silk cloth; — buttons woven like mohair; — a colour in pigeons.
more: n. m. بافتن—S. वे to weave. ( In Comp ). Woven; wrought, as zar-bāftah , or zar-baft wrought in gold, embroidered. A kind of silk cloth.
part. pass. (of بافتن) 1. Woven. 2. s. m. A kind of cloth. 3. A colour in pigeons. 4. Buttons woven like mohair.

def: s.m. = bāgh, q.v. s.m. Tiger (syn. sher); lion
more: n. m. S. व्याघ्र, Pr. vaggho . A tiger ( sher ); a lion. bāgh bakrī, sher bakrī , n. f. 1. The lion and the kid. Bāgh-bakrī ek ghāṭ pānī pīte haiṅ . Prov. The lion and the lamb drink at the same stream. (Said of a good government). 2. A boy's game similar to our "fox and geese." bāgh kā paṭṭā , n. m. A tiger's cub.

n. f. S. वल्गा from वला to go. A rein; bridle ( lagām ). bāg-pakṛā'ī , n. f. 1. Holding the horse of the bride-groom by the dhiyanās or sons-in-law of his family, being a part of the Hindu marriage ceremony. 2. The reward paid to the above-named honorary grooms.

s. f. A rein, a bridle. باگ لينا bāg lenā, To take the rein; to curb or pull up (a horse, &c.). باگ موڙنا bāg moṛnā, To turn the reins: and, this phrase is applied to the drying up of the small-pox; thus, when the pustules begin to blacken and dry up, they say, سيتلاني باگ موڙي sītlā ne bāg moṛī, or ماتاني باگ موڙي mātā ne bāg moṛī. باگ هاتهـ سي چهوٿنا bāg hāth se chhūṭnā, The reins to have slipped from the hand, denotes the loss of choice, power or controul.
subs: s.f. Tiger and goat; lion and kid; — a boy's game (similar to our 'fox and geese')
bāg-nak, s.m. Tiger's claw (set in silver and worn as a charm); a weapon made of steel, resembling huge claws, and fitting upon the hands (cf. baghnā and bag-nahā).

def: s.f. Rein; bridle ( = lagām)
subs: (-kī), lit. 'To lift the rein'; to give (a horse, &c.) the rein; to set (a horse) at full gallop
s.f. Holding the horse of a bridegroom by the sons-in-law of his family (part of a Hindū marriage ceremony); — the reward or present given to those who hold the bridegroom's horse
s.f. A long rope with which horses are led; halter
To give a loose rein (literally and figuratively); to give the rein, let (a horse) out, set (a horse) at full gallop; to leave one to himself
(-kī), To rein in (a horse, &c.); to curb, check, restrain, control
v.n. To turn the reins; turn away or back; to take a turn, to dry up (the pustules of small-pox)
(-kī), To lose control or power (over); to get beyond control; to lose a chance or opportunity
(-kī), To let the rein loose, &c. = bāg ḍhīlī karnā, &c.

def: s.m. Heron; crane ( = bak; baglā; q.q.v.)
more: See s. بک bak. بگپانت bagpānti, s. f. A row or line of cranes. بگ چال bag-chāl, s. f. Walking slowly (like a crane).
subs: s.f. Line or row of cranes
s.f. Slow, measured walk (like a crane's)
(S. vaka+rājā), s.m. King of the cranes

def: s.m. Garden, orchard, grove, cluster of trees, plantation (syn. bāṛī)
more: n. m. S. bāṭika , Z. baë . 1. A garden; orchard; a small garden. 2. A cluster of trees; plantation; grove; tope.

s. m. A garden, orchard, grove. باغ باغ هوناياهوجانا bāg̠ẖ bāg̠ẖ honā yā hojānā, v. n. To be delighted. باغ سبزدکهانايادکهلانا bāg̠ẖ-i-sabz dikhānā yā dikhlānā, To excite desire and expectation by deceitful promises.
subs: s.f. Ornamental trees and flowers of talc, &c. carried in wedding processions ( = ārāʼish); (met.) Children, family, offspring
adj. Rejoicing, delighted; — bāg̠-bāg̠ honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To be delighted, to rejoice exceedingly
(and sabz bāg̠ dikhānā), To excite desire and expectation by deceitful promises (lit. 'to exhibit a blooming garden, as by legerdemain'); to delude, deceive, cheat
s.f. Gardening, garden cultivation, business of a gardener
s.m. Owner of a garden; gardener ( = bāg̠-bān).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian باغ (bāġ).

def: s.m. Lord; master ( = beg).
Rus. n. f. 1. See ārāish (2). 2. ( Met ). Children; offspring; family; posterity. bāg bāg honā , v. n. To rejoice or exult greatly; leap with joy.
n. m. bā/?/gīchī , n. f. A small garden in which flowers, fruits, or vegetables are grown ( bāṛī ).
more: (dimin. of باغ) s. m. A small garden, a private garden.
